pear.traits: Hips wider than bust/shoulders, defined waist, narrower upper body.
pear.goal: Balance lower body volume, add width/interest up top.

bottoms: A-line skirts in solid dark tones
avoid: skinny bottoms with plain tight tops
tapered pants with baggy tops
hip-heavy embellishment (patch pockets, pleats at hip)

occasion_notes.formal: A-line gown or fit-and-flare dress with statement neckline/sleeve, minimal skirt detailing.
occasion_notes.casual: Boat-neck top + straight leg jeans + statement earrings.
occasion_notes.party: Off-shoulder top + dark A-line skirt or fit-and-flare dress.

apple.traits: Fuller midsection/bust, less defined waist, slimmer legs.
apple.goal: Create vertical line, define waist softly, draw eye to legs/neckline.

outerwear: single-button blazers that hit just below waist
avoid: tight-fitting tops that cling to midsection
high necklines with heavy detail
belts cinched tightly at natural waist

occasion_notes.casual: Wrap top + straight leg jeans, statement necklace to draw eye up.
occasion_notes.party: Fit-and-flare dress with V-neck and under-bust definition.

hourglass.traits: Bust and hips balanced, well-defined waist.
hourglass.goal: Highlight waist, maintain balance, avoid hiding shape.

avoid: boxy oversized silhouettes that hide the waist
shapeless shifts
drop-waist dresses

occasion_notes.formal: Belted or fitted sheath/mermaid gown that follows the waistline.
occasion_notes.casual: Wrap top + high-waisted jeans, thin belt.
occasion_notes.party: Bodycon or wrap dress with waist definition.

rectangle.traits: Bust, waist, hips similarly aligned, minimal waist definition.
rectangle.goal: Create the illusion of curves and a defined waist.

bottoms: jeans with back-pocket detailing for curve illusion
dresses: dress with waist seam and skirt volume
avoid: straight shift dresses with no waist seam
boxy unstructured tops paired with straight bottoms

occasion_notes.formal: Belted gown or peplum-detail dress to create waist illusion.
occasion_notes.party: Fit-and-flare dress with waist seam and volume skirt.

inverted_triangle.goal: Soften shoulder line, add volume/interest below waist.

avoid: shoulder pads, puff sleeves, boat necks
embellished/bright tops with plain bottoms

occasion_notes.formal: A-line gown, simple bodice, volume in the skirt.
occasion_notes.casual: V-neck top + flared jeans or A-line skirt.
occasion_notes.party: Fit-and-flare dress with plain top half, detailed hem.

trapezoid.traits: Shoulders broader than waist, athletic/balanced build — considered the benchmark shape.
trapezoid.goal: Maintain balance, fitted (not baggy) silhouettes work well.

suits: classic fitted two-piece suit, single or double breasted both work
avoid: overly baggy fits that hide the natural shoulder-to-waist taper

occasion_notes.formal: Fitted two-piece suit, minimal padding needed.
occasion_notes.party: Slim shirt (solid or subtle pattern) + tailored trousers.

inverted_triangle.traits: Broad shoulders/chest, narrower waist and hips.
inverted_triangle.goal: Balance the upper body, avoid excess shoulder emphasis.

tops: V-neck or crew neck (avoid boat neck/wide collars)
bottoms: straight-leg or slightly relaxed trousers to add lower-body volume
jeans with some leg volume, not skinny
suits: single-breasted jacket, natural shoulder (no heavy padding)
avoid: shoulder pads, epaulettes, horizontal stripes across chest
skinny-fit trousers that exaggerate the taper

occasion_notes.formal: Single-breasted suit, natural shoulder line, straight trouser.
occasion_notes.party: Solid-color shirt, untucked or half-tucked, straight trousers.

rectangle.traits: Shoulders, waist, hips similarly aligned, minimal definition.
rectangle.goal: Create the illusion of structure and shoulder-to-waist taper.

suits: structured shoulder, slightly tapered waist jacket
avoid: fully unstructured, boxy fits with no shape at all

occasion_notes.formal: Structured-shoulder suit with tapered waist for definition.
occasion_notes.party: Textured or patterned shirt to add visual dimension.

triangle.goal: Broaden shoulders visually, keep lower half streamlined.

tops: light/bright colors or patterns up top
bottoms: straight or slim dark trousers/jeans (no pleats, no tapering that clings to hips)
avoid: skinny jeans that emphasize hip width
plain dark tops with bright/patterned bottoms

occasion_notes.formal: Structured-shoulder suit jacket, dark slim (not skinny) trouser.
occasion_notes.casual: Striped or patterned shirt + straight dark jeans.
occasion_notes.party: Bright/patterned shirt on top, solid dark trousers.

oval.goal: Elongate the torso, avoid clinging fabrics at midsection.

tops: shirts with a straight hem worn untucked
bottoms: straight-fit trousers, mid-rise (not low-rise which emphasizes midsection)
suits: single-breasted, notch lapel, one-button jacket to elongate torso
avoid: tight/clingy fabrics at the waist
double-breasted jackets buttoned up
horizontal stripes across the midsection
tucked-in tight shirts

occasion_notes.formal: Single-button, notch-lapel suit, vertical pinstripe if patterned.
occasion_notes.casual: Untucked straight-hem shirt + mid-rise straight trousers.
occasion_notes.party: Open jacket/cardigan over solid dark tee, straight trousers.

usage_notes.purpose: Deterministic lookup table for shape -> recommended/avoid garment logic. Use as ground truth for filtering the mock clothing catalog and for validating/constraining any LLM-generated styling copy (rationale text only, not the recommendation itself).
extend_with: fabric/pattern tags per catalog item for finer matching
